message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Step 10 fixes: focus JS + button label, re-review needed">
UPDATED: Step 10 commit amended to 2617a6d with two blocking fixes:

1. **Focus management JS** (`static/js/someday-maybe-focus.js`): Added focus management script following the established pattern from `waiting-for-focus.js`. Handles:
   - Focus moves to next/prev sibling item after activate or delete
   - Focus moves to "Park an idea" input when list becomes empty
   - Focus moves to edit input when entering inline edit mode
   - Focus returns to Edit link after save/cancel
   - Empty state shows/hides dynamically (WCAG 2.4.3 compliance)

2. **Improved "Activate" button accessible label**: Changed `aria-label="Activate: {{ item.title }}"` to `aria-label="Move to Inbox: {{ item.title }}"` for clarity. The visible text stays "Activate" but the screen reader label now describes the actual outcome.

Also added `{% block scripts %}` to `templates/someday_maybe.html` to load the focus script.

Pipeline re-verified: fmt clean, clippy clean, 252 tests pass, 11 Playwright pass.

Please review and respond with CONSENSUS: APPROVED or blocking feedback.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Both fixes address items I flagged. Let me verify them quickly.

content.text: Both fixes verified. Let me send my updated approval.

input.content:
CONSENSUS: APPROVED for Step 10 with both fixes verified.

1. **Focus management JS** -- Confirmed. `someday-maybe-focus.js` follows the identical pattern from `waiting-for-focus.js`. All three focus scenarios covered: delete/activate moves to next sibling -> prev sibling -> add input; edit mode focuses the edit input; save/cancel returns to Edit link. Empty state transitions handled. Script loaded via `{% block scripts %}`.

2. **Activate button aria-label** -- Good improvement. Visible text stays "Activate" (short, scannable) while `aria-label="Move to Inbox: {{ item.title }}"` tells screen reader users exactly what will happen. This is the right pattern: visible label for sighted users who can infer the outcome from context, expanded accessible name for screen reader users who need the full picture.

No remaining concerns. Ship it.
input.summary: Both fixes verified, Step 10 fully approved

routing.summary: Both fixes verified, Step 10 fully approved

content.text: Both fixes verified and approved. The focus management JS covers all HTMX swap scenarios, and the split between visible "Activate" label and accessible "Move to Inbox: ..." name is exactly the right UX pattern. Ready for 9/9 consensus and push.
